name: Fried Lumpia
recipeInstructions:
1. To make the sweet-sour sauce, combine vinegar, 1 teaspoon soy sauce, sugar, water, ginger, and garlic in a small saucepan. Bring to a boil.
2. Stir in the cornstarch paste and simmer for 5 minutes, or until thickened. Season with salt to taste. Keep warm.
3. For the lumpia filling, mix ground pork and shrimp in a bowl. Add minced mushrooms, diced jicama, finely chopped green onions, and 2 tablespoons soy sauce. Mix well.
4. Shape about 1 1/2 tablespoons of meat mixture into a strip and place along one side of a lumpia wrapper. Roll tightly, folding in the wrapper ends while rolling. Moisten the edges lightly with water to seal. Repeat with remaining filling.
5. Heat oil in a deep pan or pot to at least 375°F (190°C).
6. Fry the lumpia in batches until golden brown, about 2-3 minutes per side.
7. Serve hot with sweet-sour sauce.
